title: Oil Falls Below $100, But Gas Prices Keep Climbing: These 4 Stocks Are Winning
author: Piero Cingari
description: Crude oil fell below $96 per barrel while gasoline prices climbed to $4.56 per gallon, creating exceptional profit margins for oil refiners. The 3-2-1 crack spread reached $56.22 per barrel—its highest level since June 2022—as refiners benefit from the widening gap between falling crude costs and stable pump prices. Major refiners reported strong first-quarter earnings that significantly beat consensus estimates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF tracking refiner performance closed at $49.15, within 5% of its all-time high of $53 and roughly 75% above its 52-week low, benefiting from exceptional refining margins.
Reported Q1 adjusted earnings of $1.65 per share, more than double the 75-cent consensus estimate, with adjusted EBITDA of $2.76 billion. Expanded share buyback authorization by $5 billion.

Reported Q1 adjusted earnings of $4.22 per share, 33% above $3.16 consensus, reversing prior-year loss. Refining segment operating income surged to $1.8 billion from $530 million loss. Raised dividend 6%.

Posted adjusted earnings of 49 cents per share against 39-cent consensus loss—an 88-cent swing. Realized refining margin jumped to $10.11 per barrel from $6.81 year-over-year. Stock rose 6.7% on results.

Reported adjusted earnings of 69 cents per share against 7-cent consensus, reversing 27-cent prior-year loss. Adjusted refinery gross margin rose 9% to $9.95 per barrel with net income of $648 million.

title: Trump's Iran Strategy Is Redrawing the Map for Oil -- 3 Energy Stocks Built to Outlast the Headlines
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: The Iran conflict and subsequent ceasefire created volatility in energy markets, but three energy stocks are well-positioned for long-term growth. Archrock benefits from increased U.S. LNG export demand with long-term contracts. Ovintiv is aggressively returning cash to shareholders while capitalizing on strong oil prices. HF Sinclair profits from refining margin spreads regardless of oil price direction.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Fee-based infrastructure business with 85% of 2026 capacity contracted and multiyear visibility. Insulated from commodity swings with ~6-year contracts. Benefits from structural increase in U.S. LNG exports projected to rise from 15.1 to 18.1 billion cubic feet per day by 2027.

Concentrated operations in high-margin Permian and Montney basins with strong cost structure. Returning at least 75% of free cash flow to shareholders with $3 billion repurchase program. Current oil prices above $90/barrel provide substantial free cash flow even with price retreats.

Refiner positioned to profit from crude-to-refined product spreads in both rising and falling oil price environments. Operates 678,000 barrels per day capacity across seven refineries. 2026 acquisition of Industrial Oils Unlimited expanded higher-margin specialties segment.

title: Trump's Iran War Sends Gasoline To Biggest Monthly Surge Since 1967 — 6 Energy Stocks To Watch
author: Piero Cingari
description: Gasoline prices surged 21.2% in March 2026, the largest monthly increase since 1967, driven by disruptions to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran war. National average gas prices jumped from $2.98 to $4.15 per gallon in six weeks. Goldman Sachs upgraded several refiner stocks as beneficiaries of elevated crack spreads and tighter energy supply chains, while economists debate whether this represents a temporary shock or a sustained inflationary regime.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Goldman Sachs maintained BUY rating; company has the most refineries eligible for small refinery exemptions with 400-600mn RIN units potentially exempt annually, providing significant cost advantages in high-RIN-price environments.
Goldman Sachs upgraded to BUY with $55 price target; benefits from small refinery exemptions worth up to $469mn and free cash flow inflection, with 12% consensus EBITDA upside through 2027-28.
Goldman Sachs maintained BUY rating with $264 price target; preferred large-cap West Coast expression with ~18% capacity in PADD 5 (~550,000 b/d) to benefit from elevated crack spreads.
Goldman Sachs upgraded to BUY with $77 price target; benefits from West Coast tightness, Hawaii jet fuel exposure, small refinery exemptions, and 15% consensus EBITDA upside through 2027-28.
Goldman Sachs maintained BUY rating with $258 price target; positioned to benefit from widening light-heavy crude differentials with highest EPS sensitivity and Venezuelan crude recovery exposure.

Goldman Sachs maintained NEUTRAL rating with $49 price target; while 32% PADD 5 capacity provides some benefit, uncertainty around Martinez restart and elevated capex (85% of operating cash flow vs. 45% peer average) limit upside.

title: Gas Tops $4, Diesel Has Its Best Month Ever — Why These Refiner Stocks Can't Stop Printing Money
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. gasoline prices surged to $4.02 per gallon and diesel hit $5.45, driven by Iran war disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz. Oil refiners are capitalizing on widened crack spreads (now ~$47/barrel vs. $20 pre-war), with refiner stocks posting exceptional gains. The VanEck Oil Refiners ETF (CRAK) is up 29% YTD on a 14-week winning streak, while individual refiners like Par Pacific and PBF Energy gained 50% and 41% in March respectively. Analysts raised price targets on Valero Energy, citing potential structural shifts in refining profitability.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ETF up 29% YTD with 14-week winning streak at all-time highs, benefiting from elevated crack spreads and global refining capacity constraints.
Leading performer with 49.75% monthly return; high-beta small-cap refiner benefits directly from margin expansion.
Up 41.26% in March; pure-play refiner with high sensitivity to crack spread movements.
Up 22.30% MTD with multiple analyst price target increases (Raymond James to $290, BofA to $247, Goldman Sachs to $237); 98% capacity utilization captures margin expansion.
Up 23.69% in March; benefits from elevated crack spreads and strong refining margins.
Up 19.75% in March; participating in sector-wide gains from improved refining profitability.
Up 27.35% in March; benefiting from elevated crack spreads and tight global refining capacity.
Up 20.80% in March; participating in refiner sector outperformance.

title: Trump Promised $2 Gas. It's Now $4 And These 5 Stocks Are Cashing In
author: Piero Cingari
description: Gas prices have surged to $3.98/gallon, nearly double Trump's $2 promise, due to the Iran war. Diesel has jumped 43% to $5.38 nationally and over $7 in California. Consumer sentiment has declined sharply as inflation expectations rise. Energy companies and refiners are posting strong gains, with SM Energy up 44% and PBF Energy up 40% month-to-date, benefiting from wider profit margins.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Leading gainer at +44% month-to-date as a pure-play Permian and Midland Basin producer with leveraged exposure to rising crude prices.
Surged 40% month-to-date as an independent refiner benefiting from widening crack spreads (margins between crude input and refined product prices) during supply disruptions.
Up 28% month-to-date, benefiting from elevated crude prices and energy sector strength amid geopolitical tensions.
Up 28% month-to-date as a refiner and energy producer capitalizing on higher fuel prices and refining margins.
Gained 26% month-to-date as a major refiner benefiting from expanded crack spreads and elevated energy prices.
